Sama Creek Industries PJSC Privacy Notice and Retention & MNPI Handling Addendum (UAE onshore/Free Zone context).
We process personal data under UAE Federal Decree-Law No. 45 of 2021 (PDPL) and, where applicable, DIFC/ADGM regimes for group operations and vendor due diligence.
Categories include role-related HR and stakeholder data, Emirates ID details (e.g., EID-0000-0000-0000), passport copy where permitted, family-origin information only if required by law, and trade licence extracts for counterparties.
Lawful bases: contract performance and legal obligation; examples include employment onboarding, SCA-related issuer compliance, and ADX/DFM disclosure record-keeping.
DSARs may be submitted via privacy@sama-creek.example; we verify requests using masked Emirates ID and trade licence references (e.g., TL-AD-XXXXXX) and limit responses to the requesting data subject.
Cross-border transfers: limited to Dhalaat Data Services Ltd (ADGM) and Arzub Cloud FZ-LLC (UAQ Free Zone) under PDPL Article 22 and applicable adequacy/contractual safeguards; no onward transfer to non-adequate jurisdictions without additional measures.
Public vs non-public status: information already disclosed via ADX/DFM market announcements, SCA-approved prospectuses, or the company’s press releases is treated as public; stale 2022–2023 operational updates are not MNPI.
Selective disclosure is prohibited; only published information may be shared with analysts, and queries must be routed to ir@sama-creek.example with a contemporaneous disclosure assessment.
Blackout windows apply to designated insiders in line with ADX trading calendar and internal policy; dealing approvals are recorded via the compliance portal and not granted during pre-results periods.
Until the SCA filing is submitted and posted on ADX, the issuer’s unannounced Q2 earnings guidance revision and related board resolution are restricted and constitute MNPI; designated insiders remain in blackout until public dissemination.
For clarity, the warehouse capacity expansion in Al Maraya Industrial Park was announced on 10 January 2023 by press release and ADX notice, and is not MNPI.
Supplier platform migration and routine IT maintenance reports are internal operational updates and are not issuer-level market-sensitive information.
Retention: Emirates ID and KYC records are retained for up to 3 years post-relationship unless a longer statutory period applies; trade licence copies and disclosure logs are kept for 6 years for audit and SCA inquiries.
We minimise processing of family-origin data; if captured to meet labour or sanctions screening requirements, it is limited to purpose, access-logged, and deleted at the earliest lawful opportunity.
Security controls include encryption at rest/in transit, access segregation for MNPI repositories, and quarterly audits of ADGM/Free Zone processors against contractual technical and organisational measures.
The current retention schedule (v1.3, effective 01 Jan 2024) is available at www.sama-creek.example/pdpl-retention and will be updated upon any material regulatory change.
